quotes.text: It's a good thing to be laughed at. It's better than to be ignored.
quotes.speaker: Harold Macmillan
quotes.context: The British Prime Minister, in a handwritten note to the Postmaster General who wanted to take action against the satirical TV program 'That Was The Week That Was' - a disarmingly self-deprecating shrug at being mocked.

quotes.text: I once spent forty-five minutes in a budget negotiation arguing about whether a particular line item was classified as a program or an initiative. I am not proud of this. The Republic survived.
quotes.speaker: Paul Ryan
quotes.context: Q&A session following a policy lecture at the American Enterprise Institute, Washington, D.C., January 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Ryan's wonkish self-awareness and his habit of leaning into budget process minutiae as humor are well documented. The giveaway is the slightly too-tidy 'The Republic survived' closer — Ryan's real punchlines tend to trail off rather than land cleanly.

quotes.text: I'll be honest with you — the first time I had to sit through a full markup session on the farm bill, I thought, this is either the most important thing I will ever do or I have made a catastrophic career error. Possibly both.
quotes.speaker: Amy Klobuchar
quotes.context: Interview with Politico's Off Message podcast, recorded in Washington, D.C., March 2019
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Klobuchar's Midwestern self-deprecation and her genuine deep history with agricultural policy make this feel completely natural. The subtle giveaway is that she'd more likely name a specific bill she championed rather than leave it at 'farm bill' as a punchline.

quotes.text: How can you govern a country that has two hundred and forty-six varieties of cheese?
quotes.speaker: Charles de Gaulle
quotes.context: The French president and WWII Free France leader, expressing wry exasperation at the ungovernability of France; recorded in Ernest Mignon's 1962 collection Les Mots du General.

quotes.text: I did not grow up thinking, one day I will master the intricacies of the Clean Air Act. And yet here I am. Here we all are. This is what public service looks like up close — a little less inspiring than the poster.
quotes.speaker: John Kerry
quotes.context: Keynote remarks at the Yale School of the Environment graduation ceremony, New Haven, Connecticut, May 2021
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Kerry's self-deflating patrician humor and his genuine ownership of environmental policy as a legacy issue fit this squarely. The subtle tell is 'a little less inspiring than the poster,' which is slightly more self-aware and punchy than Kerry typically lands.

quotes.text: I had hoped to be back here this week under different circumstances, running for re-election. But you know the old saying: you win some, you lose some. And then there's that little-known third category.
quotes.speaker: Al Gore
quotes.context: Opening his 2004 Democratic National Convention address with a self-deprecating joke about his contested 2000 presidential loss.
